_comment: Post-shrink CI-topology timing OBSERVATION (mission ci-topology-shrink-01KWQAVX, WP05 / NFR-001 / C-006). This is an ACCEPTANCE OBSERVATION artifact, not a standing timing unit gate. IMPORTANT HONESTY NOTE: a live post-shrink `run_all` measurement cannot exist pre-merge — WP03's ci-quality.yml de-serialization has not yet executed a real CI run (the mission is unmerged). The `post_shrink_projection` block below is a STRUCTURAL projection derived from WP03's de-serialization, EXPLICITLY LABELED as such — it is NOT dressed as a measurement. `measured_source_run_id` is null; the live number must be backfilled by a reviewer/operator from THIS PR's own ci-quality CI run (the first real post-shrink `run_all`). Schema mirrors tests/architectural/ci_topology_census.json `timings_baseline`.

baseline._note: Committed pre-mission baseline, copied verbatim from ci_topology_census.json `timings_baseline` (WP01). fast+arch ran SERIALIZED via arch-adversarial's `needs: fast-tests-core-misc` edge, summing to the ~29.4-min core-misc critical path.

post_shrink_projection._note: STRUCTURAL projection (NOT a measurement). WP03 dropped arch-adversarial's `needs: fast-tests-core-misc` edge and its dorny filter `if:` gate, so the always-on architectural pole runs in PARALLEL from t=0 instead of appended as a serialized ~12.3-min tail. The core-misc critical path therefore collapses from `sum(fast_core_misc, arch)` to `max(fast_core_misc_shard, arch_shard)`.
post_shrink_projection.projection_basis: WP03 arch-pole de-serialization (needs edge dropped); live measurement pending PR CI
post_shrink_projection.measured_note: BACKFILLED from PR #2391 ci-quality run 28731385584 (green). Measured core-misc critical path = 14.4 min = the de-serialized arch-adversarial pole (now the single longest core-misc job; fast_core_misc shards max 12.0, integration max 7.6, pipeline wall-clock 16.3). Reduction 29.4 -> 14.4 = 51.0%. The de-serialization worked (arch runs parallel-from-t0, no serialized tail); the pole came in HEAVIER than the ~12.3 projection, so 14.4 sits just OVER the ~13.6 next-lane sub-target while staying UNDER the 16.17-min 55% bound.
post_shrink_projection.arch_pole_execution: de-serialized, parallel-from-t0 (was: serialized tail via needs edge)
post_shrink_projection.arch_pole_is_bottleneck: HISTORICAL (true prior to mission ci-health-charter-path-and-arch-shard-01KWRTB2): arch-adversarial (14.4 min) was the single un-sharded critical-path job; matrix-sharding it (like fast-tests-core-misc) was tracked as the P1 follow-up #2397. #2397 is now closed by that mission's 3-shard split — see `arch_shard_split` below for the current (post-split) state; the 14.4-min figure remains accurate as the pre-split measurement.
post_shrink_projection.docs_only_trim: On a docs-only PR the always-on pole runs only docs-relevant guards (-m docs_scoped), cutting docs-PR arch cost from ~14.4 min to seconds while preserving doc-terminology/doc-scanning coverage; code PRs run the full pole (14.4 min).

verdict.target_rule: post-shrink core-misc critical path <= 55% x 29.4 (<=16.17 min) AND <= next-longest lane (~13.6 min) => effective ceiling ~= 13.6 min
verdict.structural_assessment: MET on both bounds as of mission ci-health-charter-path-and-arch-shard-01KWRTB2's own measurement: the binding 55% bound (14.4 <= 16.17, historical) AND the aspirational ~13.6-min next-lane sub-target, now also MET — see `arch_shard_split` for the live post-split measurement (slowest shard 9.63 min).
verdict.measured_verdict: The BINDING NFR-001 gate (<=55% x 29.4 = 16.17 min) is MET at 14.4 min (historical, PR #2391). The ~13.6 next-lane sub-target, previously narrowly missed because the un-sharded arch-adversarial pole measured 14.4 min, is now ALSO MET: mission ci-health-charter-path-and-arch-shard-01KWRTB2 (#2397) matrix-split the pole and measured a 9.63-min slowest shard on its own first CI run (PR #2405, run 28741577539) — see `arch_shard_split`. The core-misc critical path is now 12.22 min, dominated by the fast lane, not arch.
verdict.rationale: De-serialization removed the serialized arch tail from the core-misc critical path (29.4 -> 14.4, 51%). The pole then ran parallel-from-t0 as the longest single job (14.4 min, over the ~13.6-min sub-target). Mission #2397 closed that gap by matrix-sharding the pole into 3 shards; the slowest (9.63 min) is now under the sub-target and no longer the pipeline's longest parallel job — see `arch_shard_split`.
verdict.honesty_flag: BACKFILLED with live measurements from two missions: PR #2391 run 28731385584 (de-serialization) and PR #2405 run 28741577539 (3-shard split, mission ci-health-charter-path-and-arch-shard-01KWRTB2). Both the binding gate and the aspirational sub-target are now met with real CI evidence, not projections.

c006_nightly_decision.trigger_rule: Evaluate a THIN nightly-schedule option ONLY IF the (measured) PR critical path is still > ~15 min after the shrink.
c006_nightly_decision.basis: MEASURED PR critical path 14.4 min (run 28731385584) was under the 15-min trigger; the subsequent #2397 arch-pole 3-shard split (mission ci-health-charter-path-and-arch-shard-01KWRTB2, PR #2405 run 28741577539) lowered the core-misc critical path further to 12.22 min (now dominated by the fast lane). NO thin-nightly is needed at either point. The shrink satisfies #1933's INTENT — fast, targeted PR CI — without a new schedule surface.
c006_nightly_decision.intent_1933: Fast, targeted PR CI via path-filtered per-module jobs; full-suite coverage remains reachable via escape hatches + nightly over-cover.
escape_hatches_present: workflow_dispatch input `run_all` (forces every per-module job regardless of path filters)
`unmatched` fail-closed catch-all: any src/** path matched by NO named dorny group forces every job (FR-010) — the sub-T_LOC catch-all tail
c006_nightly_decision.escape_hatches_honesty_note: Correction: `ci:full` / `ready-for-ci` DO exist in ci-quality.yml, but as draft/WIP-suppression overrides (they gate whether draft-PR suites run), NOT as `run_all` full-coverage hatches. The full-coverage escape hatches are the four listed above (workflow_dispatch run_all/run_extended, the nightly schedule cron, and the unmatched fail-closed catch-all).

arch_shard_split._note: BACKFILLED from this mission's own first CI run (PR #2405, run 28741577539, green). Mission ci-health-charter-path-and-arch-shard-01KWRTB2 (#2397) matrix-split the single un-sharded arch-adversarial pole (measured 14.4 min, PR #2391 run 28731385584) into 3 marker-routed shards (`arch_shard_1/2/3`, assignment table `tests/_arch_shard_map.py`), keeping the same always-on/group-less/de-serialized job shape. Superseding the earlier structural projection with a live measurement — same honesty discipline as `post_shrink_projection` above.
arch_shard_split.partition_basis: def-test-count proxy bin-packing over the 4 pole roots (tests/adversarial, tests/architectural, tests/architecture, tests/lint) at whole-file granularity, target split 216/215/215 (data-model.md) — the imbalance in the measured durations below (vs. the near-even 216/215/215 def-count split) confirms the def-count proxy is not a true duration proxy, as research.md R2 already flagged; a future rebalance could narrow the 6m46s-9m38s spread, but all three are already comfortably under target.
arch_shard_split.locally_observed_collection_split: Local --collect-only against each shard's full-selection marker expression ('arch_shard_N and not windows_ci and (git_repo or integration or architectural)') on the implementation branch collected 248/241/317 = 806 total node IDs. Node-ID counts diverge from the 216/215/215 def-count proxy because parametrized tests expand to multiple node IDs per def. Partition completeness (union = full universe, no gaps, no double-assignment) is enforced independently by test_arch_shard_marker_completeness.py and test_shard_universe_bounded.py, not by this observation.
arch_shard_split.measured_note: BACKFILLED from PR #2405 ci-quality run 28741577539 (green): arch_shard_1=7m22s, arch_shard_2=6m46s, arch_shard_3=9m38s. Slowest shard (arch_shard_3, 9.63 min) is well under the ~13.6-min next-lane sub-target (down from the pre-split 14.4 min, a 33% reduction on the slowest-shard figure). The fast-tests-core-misc lane's longest shard (specify-cli-rest) measured 12m13s (12.22 min) on this same run, so the core-misc critical path is now max(12.22, 9.63) = 12.22 min, DOMINATED BY THE FAST LANE, not arch-adversarial — the arch pole is no longer the pipeline bottleneck at all. Pipeline wall-clock for the full run: ~13.1 min (12:58:46-13:11:54 UTC).
arch_shard_split.expected_outcome: MET. The slowest of the 3 shards (9.63 min) replaced the single 14.4-min arch-adversarial pole as measured, landing under the ~13.6-min next-lane sub-target and no longer the core-misc critical path's longest parallel-from-t0 job (that's now fast-tests-core-misc/specify-cli-rest at 12.22 min).

fr009_no_new_blind_spot.assessment: CONFIRMED — no new blind spot introduced by WP03.
fr009_no_new_blind_spot.run_all_over_cover_intact: The `changes` job's 28 per-group outputs all retain the identical `(inputs.run_all || steps.unmatched.outputs.unmatched == 'true') && 'true' || steps.filter.outputs.<group>` shape, so `run_all` (workflow_dispatch) still forces every worklist-dir job to run and over-cover every mapped group.
fr009_no_new_blind_spot.sub_t_loc_catch_all_tail: The `unmatched` fail-closed signal is untouched: any src path matched by no named group (including sub-T_LOC / newly added dirs) still forces every job — the arch pole's fail-closed catch-all coverage (the `changes` unmatched signal) is explicitly preserved per WP03's own annotation.
fr009_no_new_blind_spot.arch_pole_coverage_preserved: arch-adversarial still emits --cov=src/specify_cli + --cov=src/mission_runtime and its coverage-arch-adversarial-<shard>.xml is glob-consumed by the aggregator (enforced by WP05's test_coverage_topology_ownership.py, FR-006).
